summary: iter59 — Share/Public mini-apps + Resend transactional emails + Security audit. Backend: 18/20 pytest pass, 2 skipped (anti-abuse signup network limit). Verified: POST /api/apps/{slug}/share toggles is_public (idempotent, 404 unknown, 404 cross-tenant), GET /apps/{slug}/render anon HTML 200 + X-Frame-Options=SAMEORIGIN + CSP allowing unpkg/cdn.tailwindcss.com + connect-src 'self', public-app run by non-owner returns 200 (stranger wallet NOT debited), private+non-owner returns 403, /vibe/generate 429 after 6 calls/min, /vibe/chat 429 after ~30 calls/min, global security headers on /api/* (X-Content-Type-Options=nosniff, X-Frame-Options=DENY, HSTS, Permissions-Policy, Referrer-Policy=strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-XSS-Protection=1; mode=block), email module exports all 6 helpers, /auth/forgot-password no longer leaks reset_token when EMAIL_ENABLED=true, /waitlist returns id while firing email, admin/runtime/status owner-only (owner 200 / dev-admin 403 / anon 401). Frontend: Playwright verified /apps/:slug Share panel renders with all 8 testids, Public→Private toggle, Twitter+LinkedIn appear only when Public, share URL+embed snippet populated, iframe sandbox locked to 'allow-scripts allow-same-origin' (no allow-forms / allow-popups / allow-top-navigation), embed snippet carries the same tight sandbox.

minor.issue: Spec says anon should get 401; live implementation returns 200 and bills the owner's wallet (admin has unlimited compute so the sentinel 1e9 mask hides this). Either is acceptable per the spec footnote — pin desired contract in code or docs to avoid future ambiguity.

Public-app run by a non-owner with an invalid `inputs` payload still returns 200 with success=true and an in-band error in output.error. Consider returning 400/422 when the mini-app's own InputSchema rejects the payload so callers can distinguish billing failure from input failure.
Response body always contains the key `reset_token` (set to None when email enabled). Cleaner contract: omit the key entirely in prod mode.

issues: cdn.tailwindcss.com warns 'should not be used in production'; cloudflare beacon script blocked by the iframe CSP — expected since CSP allow-list is unpkg+tailwind only. No user-facing impact.

passed_tests: GET /api/apps/{slug}/render anon → 200 HTML, X-Frame-Options=SAMEORIGIN, CSP includes unpkg+tailwind+connect-src 'self'
POST /api/apps/{slug}/run by non-owner stranger on public app → 200; stranger wallet not debited
POST /api/apps/{slug}/run by non-owner on private app → 403
Global security headers on /api/*: nosniff, DENY, HSTS, Permissions-Policy, strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-XSS-Protection
POST /api/waitlist returns 200 with entry id (email fire-and-forget)
POST /api/auth/forgot-password returns 200 with reset_token=None (no leak when EMAIL_ENABLED=true)
Frontend: Share panel + Public/Private toggle + URL/Embed/Copy + Twitter/LinkedIn share links all present and functional
Frontend: iframe sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' (no allow-forms / allow-popups / allow-top-navigation) on both live iframe and embed snippet
skipped_tests: test_register_returns_fast_even_if_email_fails — /auth/register hit iter38 anti-abuse 'too many accounts from your network' 429 (expected protective behaviour)
action_items: [NICE-TO-HAVE] Decide and pin the anon-run-on-public-app contract: spec says 401 but implementation returns 200 and bills the owner. If 200 is the desired contract, update the spec footnote; if 401 is desired, gate /apps/{slug}/run with a require_auth dep that runs before the public-share check.
[NICE-TO-HAVE] /apps/{slug}/run could return 400/422 on InputSchema validation failure (currently 200 with success=true + in-band error string).
[NICE-TO-HAVE] /auth/forgot-password could omit the reset_token key in prod mode rather than emit `reset_token: null`.
[INFO] /auth/register has tight per-network anti-abuse (iter38) which prevented refreshing test users from this CI IP; tests fall back to dev-admin benjamin as the non-owner stranger.
critical_code_review_comments: security_middleware.SecurityHeadersMiddleware: solid OWASP defaults. The CSP whitelists 'unsafe-inline' AND 'unsafe-eval' for script-src on /render — required for Babel-standalone but worth documenting that the mini-app sandbox is the primary trust boundary (since CSP cannot stop the AI-generated JS itself).
security_middleware: HSTS is stamped on every response including non-HTTPS preview replays; browsers ignore it on HTTP so no functional risk.

email_service: send_email correctly wraps the synchronous resend SDK in loop.run_in_executor — good. It logs but never raises, satisfying the fire-and-forget contract. EMAIL_FROM uses the `taskforce.run` domain which is currently not DNS-verified in Resend, so every send returns {success:false, error:'...domain not verified...'} — INTENTIONAL until DNS is set.
apps.py share endpoint: returns 404 for both unknown and cross-tenant — privacy-by-obscurity, consistent with the rest of the API.

apps.py run on public: when the caller is anonymous and the app is public, the implementation grants the run and bills the owner. This is a coherent 'creator absorbs cost' model but the spec says 'currently 401'. Tighten one or the other.

AppViewer.jsx: iframe sandbox is correctly hardened. embed snippet exposes the SAME sandbox. The bust-cache key `iframeBust` re-triggers the GET /api/apps/{slug} fetch on every refresh — fine but creates an extra fetch per redesign; cosmetic.
AppViewer.jsx: cdn.tailwindcss.com dev warning is shown in console — production builds should ship a precompiled CSS bundle. Not blocking.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter59_share_security_emails.py (new — 20 tests, 18 pass / 2 skipped due to anti-abuse rate limit)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 8 share-panel testids present, sandbox locked, toast confirmation observed

seed_data_creation: text-capitalizer-5382f8 toggled public→ran by benjamin (a few app_runs inserted with caller_id=benjamin) → toggled back to private at end of test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter59_share_security_emails.py -v. The 2 skipped tests need a fresh IP or anti-abuse counter reset. Frontend share panel testids are: app-viewer-share-toggle, app-viewer-share-panel, app-share-public, app-share-private, app-share-url, app-share-url-copy, app-share-embed, app-share-embed-copy, app-share-twitter, app-share-linkedin. Resend domain taskforce.run is awaiting DNS verification — email sends currently log 'domain not verified' but auth flows still 200 (fire-and-forget verified). When DNS is set, no code change needed — sends will start succeeding.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. The two minor contract ambiguities (anon-run on public app returning 200 instead of 401; reset_token:null in body) are documented in action_items as nice-to-have polish, not blockers. Main agent has already live-verified the happy path; this run reproduced it deterministically in pytest.
